sections.paragraph:
ἐξέχω (s. ἔχω; Aristoph. et al.; SIG 827 III, 11 [116/17 a.d.]; LXX; ViDa 1 [p. 76, 13 Sch.]; cp. 2 Esdr 13:25, 27 ὁ πύργος ὁ ἐξέχων ἐκ τοῦ οἴκου τ. βασιλέως; Just., D. 91, 2) to be readily noticeable, stand out, be prominent, of raised places as places of honor οἱ ἐξέχοντες τόποι Mt 20:28 v.l.=ASyn. 216, 38 [Agr 22].

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 349
